reviews.review_text: Camp Nyroca is our favourite camp on the peninsula. Great quiet campground amongst beautiful gums with great facilities , clean camp kitchen and amenities. Caretaker was welcoming and catered to all our needs. We will keep coming back.

Disappointed paid for 4 nights and only stayed 2. Caretaker not engaged pointed to our site and didn’t give any information on facilities and went back inside didn’t see him again. No laundry facilities and didn’t see toilets being cleaned.  Nice spot shame burnt from a fire but can see it’s quite a peaceful place to stay.

Beautiful surroundings, reminds me of school camp when I was a kid. Nice hot showers and clean bathrooms until more campers arrived on day 3 and no cleaning of toilets, or bathrooms for our remaining 2 days.
Down side: We didn't see the caretaker until day 2 of being here because we chased him up up after seeing him about to go inside his residence. Checked ourselves in, as nobody was around.  Called the caretaker but no answer, so found our own spot to park up and set up. Lucky we figured out ourselves not to drink the water. Finally got in contact with him and he reassured us he'd come by our van that afternoon, however we did not see him again! All rubbish bins and recycling bins overflowing, bbq filthy,mens toilet bins overflowing. Day 5 and still no sight or communication with caretaker.
- Self check in
- Nothing maintained
- Left to figure out where everything is on your own.
- $35 a night for power,toilet and shower.
- Camp kitchen is good.
- Caretaker not seen except when we approached him.
Be prepared to figure it all out yourself!
☆ Not as advertised.
Lovely place, in the woods. Great facilities, a very relaxing spot. Only down side is it's beside the B100, but didn't intrude very much.

Great spot, quiet and peaceful, alongside a creek, excellent facilities with games room, bbq, camp kitchens, bathrooms with great hot showers and nice walking tracks around the property. Also dog friendly (be mindful of your dog that he/she respects the resident chicken that roams around the property). Ps; feed the chicken chocolate and he'll be your servant for life! Nice helpful Guy running the place. Easy access to Greenly beach and to Farm beach from here. Some powered sites generously spaced out. Fire pits (outside of November to mid April), tree swings everywhere. Great place for kids with bikes. We'll be back for certain!
Shower so clean

Peaceful and really spacious camp, clean bathrooms with hot showers. We had the whole place to ourselves. Friendly caretaker and even friendlier chicken who hung with us all afternoon. A short drive from coffin bay, would definitely recommend.

Such a great campsite.
Lots of amenities, games room, swings all around the sites, plenty space and not crowded.
Hot shower with amazing water pressure and very clean toilets.
Highly recommended!
We had a great couple of nights here as a work event, particularly since the upgrades. It's useful for groups with everything you'll need, and the quietness comes free...
Super cool to be able to see this on a day when the world was in turmoil

What a fabulous spot, especially if you have a tent and are sick of gravel! Just us and one other camper. Beautiful setting, quiet and out of the wind. We even saw a shooting star which we'll never forget. Loads of space, camp kitchen and clean, hot showers. Helpful caretaker on site. Breakfasr with the resident chickens. So glad we went. Give it a go! 💫
Great spot to camp (same price as a NP) Amenities, camp kitchen + BBQs were all clean. Short drive to Farm Beach

Location in the middle of nowhere, very quiet. Electricity works well, water also works well. Toilets were very clean without any issues. The games room is very, kitchen also very old and not usable but the rest was good. This place was built in 1970s.

Great showers and toilets.  Quiet location.  Friendly staff.
Sheltered from the wind.  And seriously cheap.
Typical scout camp, bit grubby, stopping to shut the gate then doing a hill start on gravel with a caravan didn't help the tyres much, but was happy to have power as it was hot

Stop here on my journey around Australia. Really underated place on map. 30mins drive from Coffin Bays. Around 20 bucks for a night ( unpower camp site) that include kitchen equipment and hot shower. The bathroom is super clean and tidy - everything is nice and tidy. The place is quiet and peaceful. Not thing i should ask more than this. Will come back and visit again.

Hidden gem. Just out of town and all you need.

Safe for families and kids.

Downside is your away from shops and beach..

This place does not need writing down as it is etched in my memory forever. For the those who like the peace and quiet together with the serenity of the bush and birds, this place (off season) is your cup of tea... guaranteed. I love everything about this place as for me it has it all. Power or unpowered  sites, all amenities, plenty of shade and open space, walk tracks, and phone and internet if you have Telstra (forget Optus here). On top of all that you have a great caretaker (Craig) who is empathetic to your needs within reason. I could write a book on this place. Dont change a thing guys... its just right as it is. Thanks for a wonderful 8 days stay. Will be back in Apr-May 2024 for a longer stay if you will allow me.

I can do nothing but rave about our weekend away.
It was just amazing.
Hot showers.
Plenty to explore
Chickens to feed the scraps to.
Great place to stay had caravan there two days good place to just enjoy and relax  dog friendly as well  very clean  and powered sites as well

Great place to stay clean tidy well kept grounds large sites powered & un power clean amenities showers / toilets plenty of walking trails dog friendly BBQ'S tables fire pits camp kitchen games room & lots more couldn't ask for a better place to chill well worth the stay will be back
